heading: Miscellaneous administrative authority
label: 332 Miscellaneous administrative authority
notes: Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center of the Department of the Treasury to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 203(4), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

References in Other Laws to GS–16, 17, or 18 Pay RatesReferences in laws to the rates of pay for GS–16, 17, or 18, or to maximum rates of pay under the General Schedule, to be considered references to rates payable under specified sections of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, see section 529 [title I, § 101(c)(1)] of , set out in a note under .Pub. L. 101–509section 5376 of Title 5

children.text: contract for the temporary or intermittent services of experts or consultants as authorized by , United States Code, at rates not to exceed the per diem equivalent to the rate for GS–18;section 3109 of title 5
contract with and reimburse the Department of State for health and medical services for employees of the Department of the Treasury and their dependents serving in foreign countries;
provide for official functions, and reception and representation activities;
maintain, repair, and clean uniforms furnished by the Department of the Treasury to uniformed employees;
provide athletic and related activities for students at the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center, Glynco, Georgia;

install and maintain fencing, lighting, guard booths, and other facilities as necessary for the performance of protective functions of the Department of the Treasury on property not owned by or under jurisdiction and control of the United States Government and, subsequently, to remove the facilities therefrom;

enter into reciprocal assistance agreements with State and local law enforcement agencies and, in connection with the agreements and otherwise, train employees of those agencies, when necessary, with or without reimbursement;
provide laboratory assistance to State and local law enforcement agencies, with or without reimbursement;
obtain insurance for official motor vehicles operated in foreign countries; and

children.label: i
children.text: acquire in foreign countries real property by lease for periods not greater than 10 years and personal property for use in foreign countries by purchase, lease, or otherwise, and

children.label: ii
children.text: manage, maintain, repair, improve, and insure by purchase of commercial insurance policies properties referred to in clause (i), and

children.label: B
children.text: when appropriate, dispose of (by sale, rent, transfer, or otherwise) properties referred to in subparagraph (A)(i).
